title: Masked-Child Surrogate Calibration for Safe EC Prefix Decisions
description: This project studies a narrow empirical question: whether a leakage-controlled masked-child surrogate can calibrate when a fixed hierarchical EC scorer should stop at an internal node instead of forcing an EC-4 prediction. The contribution is a decision-layer heuristic and transfer study, not a general solution to unseen enzyme-function prediction.
motivation: Recent EC papers already cover hierarchy-aware modeling, trustworthy hierarchical prediction, and even coarse placement for unseen enzyme functions. The remaining gap is narrower and practically important: under a known EC-3 parent, models still over-specialize into the wrong known EC-4 child when the true child is absent from the current label set. What is missing is a carefully benchmarked, leakage-controlled stopping rule and evidence about whether synthetic masked-child calibration actually transfers to temporal future-child events.
proposed_approach: Cache frozen ESM-2 embeddings, train lightweight parent-local child scorers, and add a two-stage stopping rule. First, a standard split-conformal acceptance test checks whether the best known child looks represented. Second, a masked-child surrogate stage removes one observed child per parent during calibration, rebuilds every parent-local feature that could leak the removed child, and uses the resulting score distribution to decide whether descent is safe when the parent is known but the correct child may be missing. The distinctive experiment is a parent-level transfer analysis comparing masked-child gains against temporal future-child gains and against generic hierarchical selective baselines.

related_work.title: How Not to be Seen: Predicting Unseen Enzyme Functions using Contrastive Learning
related_work.authors: Xiang Ma, Pratik Joshi, Iddo Friedberg, Qing Li
related_work.how_it_differs: This 2026 bioRxiv preprint introduces EnzPlacer, a new contrastive model for placing proteins with unseen EC-4 labels at coarser EC levels. The proposed work does not introduce a new predictor; it adds a leakage-controlled stop/go calibration layer on top of an existing hierarchical scorer and studies transfer from synthetic masked-child surrogates to temporal future-child events.

related_work.title: Trustworthy prediction of enzyme commission numbers using a hierarchical interpretable transformer
related_work.authors: Louis Dumontet, So-Ra Han, Jun Hyuck Lee, Tae-Jin Oh, Mingon Kang
related_work.how_it_differs: HIT-EC is a strong enzyme-specific hierarchical architecture for trustworthy EC prediction. The proposed work is narrower: a post hoc decision-layer heuristic for deciding when not to descend.

related_work.title: Hierarchical Contrastive Learning for Enzyme Function Prediction
related_work.authors: Soorin Yim, Doyeong Hwang, Kiyoung Kim, Sehui Han
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper improves hierarchy-aware representation learning for enzyme function prediction, including unseen settings. The proposed work does not claim better embeddings; it isolates calibration and decision-making.

related_work.title: Hierarchical Selective Classification
related_work.authors: Shani Goren, Ido Galil, Ran El-Yaniv
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper provides a general framework for predicting internal nodes under coverage constraints. The proposed work contributes an enzyme-specific masked-child surrogate and explicit transfer diagnostics to real future-child events.

related_work.title: Functional protein mining with conformal guarantees
related_work.authors: Ron S. Boger, Seyone Chithrananda, Anastasios N. Angelopoulos, Peter H. Yoon, Michael I. Jordan, Jennifer A. Doudna
related_work.how_it_differs: This work shows the value of conformal calibration in protein decision pipelines. The proposed work uses conformal calibration only for represented children and adds a separate surrogate missing-child rule.

related_work.title: EC-Bench: a benchmark for enzyme commission number prediction
related_work.authors: Saeedeh Davoudi, Christopher S. Henry, Christopher S. Miller, Farnoush Banaei-Kashani
related_work.how_it_differs: EC-Bench standardizes EC prediction tasks and evaluation, but it does not study masked-child surrogate calibration or transfer to future-child emergence.

hypothesis: Masked-child surrogate calibration will reduce overspecialization under known EC parents more effectively than generic confidence, energy, OpenMax, or hierarchical selective stopping rules, and its parent-level gains will transfer at least partially to temporal future-child events.
success_criteria: At matched coverage on temporal future-child events, reduce catastrophic overspecialization relative to both forced hierarchical prediction and the HSC-style stopping baseline.
On masked-child surrogate tests, improve correct-safe-prefix rate over energy and OpenMax baselines.
Show positive parent-level correlation between masked-child gains and temporal future-child gains, and match or exceed the transfer correlation achieved by generic hierarchical selective baselines.
Identify at least one interpretable parent-level statistic that predicts when surrogate calibration transfers well or poorly.
Complete the full study within the single-GPU 8-hour budget using one shared embedding pass, at most 12 parents, 2 seeds, and parent-local refits only.

novelty_assessment.claim: The novelty is not unseen enzyme prefix prediction in general. It is masked-child surrogate calibration as a leakage-controlled empirical decision-layer heuristic for hierarchical EC models, plus a parent-level transfer study.
closest_prior_work: How Not to be Seen: Predicting Unseen Enzyme Functions using Contrastive Learning
Trustworthy prediction of enzyme commission numbers using a hierarchical interpretable transformer
Hierarchical Contrastive Learning for Enzyme Function Prediction
Functional protein mining with conformal guarantees
novelty_assessment.why_not_already_done: The closest recent unseen-enzyme paper trains a new model for coarse placement of unseen EC labels. Existing selective-prediction papers provide generic stopping rules, but they do not construct a leakage-controlled missing-child surrogate or test parent-level transfer from synthetic masking to temporal future-child events.

calibration_scope.known_child: Standard split-conformal validity applies only when the true child label is represented in the calibration distribution.
calibration_scope.masked_child_surrogate: The masked-child threshold is valid only for the synthetic child-removal distribution induced by the rebuild protocol; it is not a theorem about arbitrary unseen enzyme functions.
calibration_scope.paper_claim: The main claim is empirical transfer. If transfer is weak, the paper should be framed as a benchmarked study of surrogate calibration limits rather than a new practical method.

experimental_design.dataset: Swiss-Prot enzymes with complete EC-4 labels, using snapshot 2018_02 for training/validation and 2023_01 for temporal future evaluation.
experimental_design.parent_selection: Using only the 2018 snapshot, keep EC-3 parents with at least 4 observed EC-4 children and at least 25 sequences per retained child, capped at 12 parents by training support.
primary_evaluations: Temporal future-child test under known EC-3 parents
Masked-child surrogate test with one removed child per parent per seed
Parent-level transfer diagnostic comparing synthetic and temporal gains
Transferability stratification based on parent statistics computed from training data

feasibility.model_scale: Frozen esm2_t30_150M_UR50D embeddings with parent-local multinomial logistic regressors on cached features.
feasibility.compute_fit: The scope is intentionally reduced to one embedding pass, at most 12 parents, 2 seeds, and parent-local retraining only, making the 8-hour single-A6000 budget plausible.
feasibility.main_risk: Masked-child gains may fail to transfer to real future-child events. The proposal makes that transfer risk the central result instead of hiding it.

surrogate_protocol.child_masking: For each selected parent and seed, remove one supported child from the parent-local label vocabulary and score that child's held-out examples against the retained siblings.
feature_rebuild: Recompute parent-local prototypes without removed-child examples
Rebuild node-restricted k-NN or exact-neighbor indices after removal
Recompute vote-mass and density features from retained supports only
Drop or rebuild any identity-based lookup whose candidate pool still contains the removed child
leakage_checks: Verify masked-child examples receive zero removed-child vote mass or identity hits
Compare fully rebuilt features against a strict no-retrieval-feature ablation
Reject any surrogate protocol where performance gains disappear after proper rebuilds
